title: Listening - Part 3: Learning from a Mistake
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a mistake they made. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker learned from the experience. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a mistake they made. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker learned from the experience. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about something they learned after making a mistake.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I booked concert tickets online as a surprise for my sister. The price was good, and I was so pleased to find seats that I paid immediately. When I gave them to her, she noticed I'd selected the same date as an important family wedding. [19]I had to pay a fee to exchange the tickets, and since then I've learned never to confirm a booking without checking dates and arrangements properly first.[/19] My sister still enjoyed the concert, but my bargain seats ended up costing far more than expected.||Speaker 2 Man: Last term I agreed to design posters for a college event because I enjoy art and thought I could finish them in one evening. I forgot I also had assignments and shifts at work that week. [20]The posters were completed late and under pressure, so now I consider how much time a job will genuinely take before agreeing that I can do it by a certain date.[/20] I still volunteer for creative tasks, but I write deadlines in my calendar and start much earlier.||Speaker 3 Woman: My laptop began making a strange noise, but I kept using it because I was busy and hoped the problem would disappear. Eventually it stopped working the night before I had to submit an important report. [21]A technician later told me the original repair would have been simple, which taught me that when something seems wrong, it's far better to get help immediately than pretend there isn't a problem.[/21] Fortunately my work was backed up, but I still had an extremely stressful evening.||Speaker 4 Man: I saw a jacket online with only one left in my size. The website said the offer would end that evening, so I bought it without thinking. When it arrived, the colour was completely unsuitable and returning it was complicated. [22]I realised I had allowed the pressure of a limited offer to make the decision for me, and now I wait and consider whether I really want something before spending money.[/22] There will nearly always be another jacket, even if an advertisement makes it sound urgent.||Speaker 5 Woman: At a café, I left my phone on the table while I went to order another drink. I thought I would be away for only a moment and my friends were nearby, but they had gone outside to take a call. When I returned, the phone had disappeared. [23]Replacing it was expensive and inconvenient, and I now keep valuables with me rather than assuming they will be safe in a public place.[/23] I was angry with whoever took it, but I also knew I had made the opportunity far too easy.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I booked concert tickets online as a surprise for my sister. The price was good, and I was so pleased to find seats that I paid immediately. When I gave them to her, she noticed I'd selected the same date as an important family wedding. I had to pay a fee to exchange the tickets, and since then I've learned never to confirm a booking without checking dates and arrangements properly first. My sister still enjoyed the concert, but my bargain seats ended up costing far more than expected.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: Last term I agreed to design posters for a college event because I enjoy art and thought I could finish them in one evening. I forgot I also had assignments and shifts at work that week. The posters were completed late and under pressure, so now I consider how much time a job will genuinely take before agreeing that I can do it by a certain date. I still volunteer for creative tasks, but I write deadlines in my calendar and start much earlier.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My laptop began making a strange noise, but I kept using it because I was busy and hoped the problem would disappear. Eventually it stopped working the night before I had to submit an important report. A technician later told me the original repair would have been simple, which taught me that when something seems wrong, it's far better to get help immediately than pretend there isn't a problem. Fortunately my work was backed up, but I still had an extremely stressful evening.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I saw a jacket online with only one left in my size. The website said the offer would end that evening, so I bought it without thinking. When it arrived, the colour was completely unsuitable and returning it was complicated. I realised I had allowed the pressure of a limited offer to make the decision for me, and now I wait and consider whether I really want something before spending money. There will nearly always be another jacket, even if an advertisement makes it sound urgent.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: At a café, I left my phone on the table while I went to order another drink. I thought I would be away for only a moment and my friends were nearby, but they had gone outside to take a call. When I returned, the phone had disappeared. Replacing it was expensive and inconvenient, and I now keep valuables with me rather than assuming they will be safe in a public place. I was angry with whoever took it, but I also knew I had made the opportunity far too easy.

options.text: to be more patient with others
to avoid making decisions in a hurry

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Booking tickets for the wrong date taught the speaker to verify details before paying.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker learned to assess available time and manage deadlines more realistically.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Ignoring a warning sign taught the speaker to seek expert help before a problem becomes serious.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: An unsuitable rushed purchase taught the speaker not to decide under pressure.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Losing a phone made the speaker more careful about keeping belongings secure in public.
